item.title: When will traffic in the Strait of Hormuz return to normal?
item.description: Ships are beginning to transit the Strait of Hormuz again after the initial U.S.-Iran agreement reopened the waterway. But traffic may not get back to normal right away. Scott Savitz, senior engineer and professor at RAND School of Public Policy, joins to discuss.

item.title: Trump's goals for the Iran war and what he's saying now
item.description: When the U.S.-Iran conflict began, President Trump laid out a litany of aggressive war aims. Here's what the president and his top aides said then — and how their views have changed.

item.title: Facing bipartisan criticism of Iran deal, Trump lashes out at "fools"
item.description: Republican Senators Bill Cassidy, Thom Tillis, Ted Cruz and Tom Cotton have been critical of the 14-point memorandum of understanding signed on Wednesday.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic ramps up in wake of U.S.-Iran deal signing
item.description: More tankers are transiting the Strait of Hormuz after 111 days of gridlock, but Iran and the U.S. still have much to discuss.

item.title: Even after a brain tumor altered this artist's sight, he crafted his greatest work yet
item.description: After artist Vincent Serritella underwent surgeries to remove a brain tumor on his optic nerve, he found a way to overcome his change in vision. Tony Dokoupil has the story.

item.title: Obama Presidential Center opens: "We wanted it to be a vibrant, living celebration of community"
item.description: Former President Barack Obama formally unveiled his presidential center in Chicago on Thursday, calling it an "expression of thanks" to the city and a monument, not to his story, but to the U.S.'s story. Caitlin Huey-Burns reports.

item.title: Ukraine launches drone attack on Moscow: "If Ukraine burns, then your Moscow will burn as well"
item.description: Ukraine hammered Russia with the largest attack on the capital city of Moscow since Russia invaded its neighbor more than four years ago. A massive swarm of Ukrainian drones hit a major oil refinery there, among other targets, on Thursday. Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said it was a retaliatory act. Aidan Stretch reports.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Luigi Mangione's legal team drops psychiatric defense in state murder trial
item.description: Attorneys for Luigi Mangione, the man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, dropped plans for a psychiatric defense in his state case on Thursday. CBS News legal reporter Katrina Kaufman has the details.

item.title: How Trump's rhetoric on Iran has evolved over the course of the war
item.description: Many of President Trump's stated reasons for going to war with Iran are left unresolved in the initial deal with Iran, and many of the president's goals for the war haven't been met. But what has changed in just a few months is his rhetoric. Margaret Brennan reports.

item.title: Democrats and some Republicans fuming at Trump's Iran deal
item.description: Vice President JD Vance defended President Trump's deal with Iran, which he signed on Wednesday at the Palace of Versailles in France. Weijia Jiang has more reaction and details.

item.title: A timeline of the Karen Read case
item.description: A look at the timeline of events in Karen Read's high-profile Massachusetts murder trial and retrial. Read was acquitted of killing her boyfriend, Boston police officer John O'Keefe.

item.title: Rep. Adriano Espaillat facing Mamdani-backed challenger in New York Democratic primary
item.description: Democratic Rep. Adriano Espaillat of New York is facing a primary challenger from his left, who is endorsed by New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani. Political strategists Erin Maguire and Chuck Rocha join with analysis.

item.title: Trump awards Medal of Honor to 3 veterans of the Vietnam, Afghanistan wars
item.description: In a ceremony at the White House, the president paid tribute to retired Marine Corps Maj. James Capers Jr., retired Army Maj. Nicholas Dockery and Marine Col. John W. Ripley, who died in 2008.

item.title: Obama Presidential Center opens in Chicago with star-studded celebrations
item.description: The Obama Presidential Center's grand opening ceremony featured a lineup of tributes from his family and closest colleagues, and musical performances by Bruce Springsteen, The Roots and more.

item.title: Luigi Mangione's legal team withdraws psychiatric defense
item.description: Luigi Mangione's legal team says they withdrew a psychiatric defense​​ in his New York state murder trial one day after telling the court they would use it.

item.title: States not required to give community-based care for those with disabilities: DOJ opinion
item.description: The Office of Legal Counsel opinion released Thursday said states aren't required by law to integrate mentally disabled patients with their peers by providing community or home-based care.

item.title: America's long history of political turmoil
item.description: In his new book, "Been There, Done That," historian Greg Jackson shines a light on dark times in U.S. history, such as insurrections, political partisanship and disregard for the Constitution. Jackson joins "The Takeout" with more.

item.title: Details after Trump signs peace deal with Iran
item.description: The clock started ticking for the U.S. and Iran to reach a final long-term peace deal after the presidents of both countries signed off on the 14-point memorandum of understanding on Wednesday. CBS News White House reporter Aaron Navarro has more.

item.title: Rep. Espaillat brushes off primary challenge: "I've been in this neighborhood for a long time"
item.description: Democratic Rep. Adriano Espaillat, who chairs the Congressional Hispanic Caucus, has been one of the fiercest critics of President Trump, dating back to the president's first term. But the New Yorker now faces a primary challenger from his left who believes he hasn't been fierce enough and also has the backing of New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani. Espaillat joins "The Takeout" to discuss.

item.title: Air Force unit faces flu outbreak weeks after vaccine mandate dropped
item.description: Dozens of service members at Lackland Air Force Base in Texas have fallen ill with the flu in the weeks since Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth rescinded the vaccine mandate, sources familiar confirmed to CBS News.

item.title: Comedian Carlos Mencia charged with tax fraud in L.A.
item.description: Carlos Mencia is accused of failing to report $8.7 million in income over a six-year period.

item.title: How the Obama Presidential Center is unique among presidential libraries
item.description: Former presidents and world leaders gathered in Chicago on Thursday to celebrate the grand opening of the Obama Presidential Center. CBS News congressional correspondent Caitlin Huey-Burns reports.

item.title: NYC celebrates Knicks NBA championship with ticker-tape parade
item.description: The New York Knicks went up New York City's famous Canyon of Heroes for a massive ticker-tape parade celebrating the 2026 NBA champions.

item.title: H. R. McMaster says preliminary Iran deal "cuts very significantly against U.S. interests"
item.description: President Trump's memorandum of understanding with Iran is being met with criticism from both Republicans and Democrats. H. R. McMaster, former national security adviser to Mr. Trump, joins with his perspective on the deal.

item.title: Initial U.S.-Iran deal draws mixed reactions from Republicans
item.description: President Trump signed a memorandum of understanding with Iran on Wednesday night. The reviews are in, and Senate Republicans were either critical or cautiously hopeful that something better could be achieved. CBS News' Weijia Jiang and Nikole Killion have the latest.

item.title: CDC issues $107 million for emergency Ebola response
item.description: The U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention set aside more than $100 million to strengthen its response to the Ebola outbreak in East and Central Africa. CBS News' Shanelle Kaul reports on the emergency funding.

item.title: 6/18: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Vice President JD Vance warns Israel to abide by initial U.S.-Iran deal; Obama Presidential Center holds star-studded grand opening ceremony.

item.title: Obama Presidential Center grand opening ceremony wraps in Chicago
item.description: The Obama Presidential Center held a star-studded grand opening in Chicago on Thursday. CBS News Chicago's Irika Sargent joins with highlights of the ceremony.

item.title: GOP congressman, missing for months, set to return on June 30
item.description: Republican Rep. Tom Kean Jr. of New Jersey will return to Congress on June 30, his spokesperson said, after being away since March in an unexplained absence that has confounded Capitol Hill.
